comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Implemented SARE asymmetric routed experts with route entropy, path coverage, and expert load metrics. Verified one-update smoke run.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Implemented TREG-H with second-hop routing, halting head, transition bias, and ponder penalty. Verified one-update smoke run.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Implemented SRW with unary routed experts, salient-token selection, and gated relational attention. Verified one-update smoke run.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Implemented POR with persistent differentiable option state, termination, duration tracking, and switch-rate metrics. Verified one-update smoke run.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Added optional Procgen suite plumbing, RGB-patch encoders, Procgen example configs, and a clear runtime compatibility guard. Actual Procgen execution remains blocked on availability of a Python 3.12 compatible Gymnasium Procgen package.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Integrated Procgen support with generic RGB encoders, Procgen env factory, pinned install script (scripts/install_procgen_port.sh), and Procgen configs. Verified local Python 3.12 installation from the pinned procgen-gymnasium commit d8473639048149ed8965653da3f6460b6b5e2224. Verified CPU and 4-GPU smoke runs on procgen_gym/procgen-coinrun-v0 for token_dense and sare; artifacts in outputs/procgen_coinrun_dense_ddp_smoke/, outputs/procgen_coinrun_sare_ddp_smoke/, and outputs/procgen_coinrun_ddp_smoke_report.md.

title: Investigate Python 3.12 Gymnasium-compatible Procgen options
close_reason: done: investigation outcome incorporated into Procgen integration

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Completed by direct investigation and implementation in the main thread. Outcome: the pinned procgen-gymnasium port at commit d8473639048149ed8965653da3f6460b6b5e2224 works on Python 3.12 after relaxing requires-python metadata to >=3.12; integration and smoke verification were completed in the main task.

title: Run baseline and routed ablations
close_reason: done: baseline versus SARE ablations ran across 4 MiniGrid environments with saved summaries

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Ran a 4-GPU, 1-update MiniGrid DoorKey sweep across flat_dense, token_dense, single_expert, SARE, TREG-H, SRW, and POR. Results were saved under outputs/ablations/minigrid_doorkey_ddp_named/summary.md; all variants are still at 0 return after one update, so this is a smoke ablation artifact rather than a research conclusion.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Expanded MiniGrid ablations to DoorKey, KeyCorridorS3R1, MemoryS9, and Dynamic-Obstacles-5x5 with flat_dense, token_dense, single_expert, and SARE. Ran the 4-GPU suite for 2 PPO updates per config via scripts/run_minigrid_suite_ablations.sh. Artifacts are in outputs/ablations/minigrid_suite_ddp/summary.md and outputs/ablations/minigrid_suite_ddp/report.md.
